meanings.definition: act as a barrier between; stand between
examples: The mountain range divides the two countries
He separated the fighting children
Moses parted the Red Sea
We distinguish several kinds of maple
divide the cake into three equal parts
The British carved up the Ottoman Empire after World War I
Separate the wheat from the chaff

meanings.definition: arrange or order by classes or categories
examples: How would you classify these pottery shards--are they prehistoric?

meanings.definition: discontinue an association or relation; go different ways
examples: The business partners broke over a tax question
The couple separated after 25 years of marriage
My friend and I split up

meanings.definition: go one's own way; move apart
examples: The friends separated after the party

meanings.definition: become separated into pieces or fragments
examples: The figurine broke
The freshly baked loaf fell apart

meanings.definition: treat differently on the basis of sex or race

examples: The two pieces that we had glued separated

meanings.definition: divide into two or more branches so as to form a fork
examples: The road forks

meanings.definition: being or feeling set or kept apart from others; ; - Sherwood Anderson
examples: she felt detached from the group
could not remain the isolated figure he had been
thought of herself as alone and separated from the others
had a set-apart feeling
a dislocated knee
a separated shoulder
a detached part
on one side of the island was a hugh rock, almost detached
the separated spacecraft will return to their home bases
